shots.prompt: Grey dawn light, exhausted Theo films the cabin: the door still shut, the chair still wedged. Theo, hollow: "Made it to morning. Door never opened. But..." He slowly tilts the phone down toward the floor. Realistic handheld found-footage, washed-out morning grain.

Phone footage of the wooden floorboards: a perfect ring is burned into the wood, faintly warm, a thread of smoke still curling up. Theo, shaken, behind camera: "It's on the floor. Inside. The same circle, burned right into the wood. It was IN here." Photoreal, eerie, dim light.

The soft hum now seems to come from the walls and the radio at once. Theo films the radio; in the curved glass of its dial, the tall figure's calm reflection appears, observing. Radio voice, gentle, mimicking him: "...not going to hurt you." Theo goes very still. Realistic, quietly terrifying.

Theo backs to the window and films the frozen lake: a huge faint geometric pattern now glows under the ice, stretching across the whole lake. Theo, awed and afraid: "The whole lake. There's something under the whole lake." Cold daylight, handheld, photoreal.

Tight, shaky selfie-cam close on Theo's face, a faint pulsing light reflected in his eyes. He speaks strangely calm now, as if being shown something. Theo: "It's showing me things. Places. It keeps showing me the lake." Realistic found-footage, screen glow, unsettling stillness.

Theo lowers the phone slightly; in the cabin doorway stands the tall figure, haloed in pale dawn light, one long hand slowly raised — not a threat, an invitation. The shot holds, breath fogging the lens. Found-footage realism, awe and dread, no music.
